topics.title: Day Trading
topics.description: Learn the essentials of day trading: fast-paced markets, risk management, and the discipline needed to trade intraday without the hype.

content.heading: What is Day Trading?
content.text: Day trading involves buying and selling financial instruments within the same trading day. All positions are closed before the market closes to avoid unmanageable overnight risk. It's a high-intensity strategy that requires significant time, focus, and a precise 'edge' over the market.

content.heading: The 90% Rule
content.text: Most day traders lose money because they treat the market like a casino. Successful day trading is about process over outcome. You don't need a 90% win rate; you need a strategy where your winners outsize your losers and the discipline to walk away when your rules aren't met.

content.heading: Essential Day Trading Tools
content.text: To succeed, you need a high-speed execution platform, a reliable data feed, and most importantly, a Trade Journal. Tracking every entry and exit allows you to see the patterns in your own behavior that lead to drawdowns.

faqs.question: Can I day trade with a small account?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you must be extremely cautious with leverage. Position sizing is critical. Trading a small account requires more discipline, not more risk.

faqs.question: How much time does day trading take?
faqs.answer: Active day trading typically requires being present during high-volume sessions, such as the London/New York overlap (1pm - 4pm GMT).

topics.title: Forex Trading
topics.description: Master the foreign exchange market. Understand currency pairs, pips, and market sessions without the guru promises of overnight wealth.

content.heading: Understanding the Forex Market
content.text: Forex is the largest and most liquid financial market in the world. It operates 24 hours a day, 5 days a week, and is driven by global economic data, interest rates, and geopolitical events.
